entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term change in weather patterns that acts as a global threat to economy and livelihoods

entities.name: GLOBAL WARMING
entities.description: The warming of the earth caused by human activities and increased concentrations of heat-trapping gases

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSION
entities.description: Emissions from human activities and food systems that contribute to global warming

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: A physical, environmental, economic, and social issue ensuring all people have access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A large agricultural producer highly vulnerable to changing climate and food production system disruptions

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production per unit area, which is negatively affected by climate change

entities.name: MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A lack of essential nutrients resulting from food instability

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality caused by the adverse effects of climate change

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that damage crops, with increased vulnerability caused by climate change

entities.name: DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions affecting crops or humans, exacerbated by climate change and floods

entities.name: FOOD SCARCITY
entities.description: A shortage of food leading to food insecurity, often caused by crop failures

entities.name: FOOD PRICES
entities.description: The market cost of food, subject to volatility due to production instability

entities.name: RISING TEMPERATURES
entities.description: An increase in global temperatures contributing to climate change

entities.name: PRECIPITATION PATTERNS
entities.description: Changes in rainfall distribution that impact food production

entities.name: DROUGHTS
entities.description: Periods of deficient rainfall causing crop failures and reduced yields

entities.name: FLOODS
entities.description: Overflow of water causing crop damage and disrupting food supply chains

entities.name: FOOD STABILITY
entities.description: The dimension of food security where access to food is maintained over time despite cyclical events

entities.name: FOOD AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The dimension of food security referring to the physical presence of food

entities.name: FOOD ACCESSIBILITY
entities.description: The dimension of food security referring to the economic and physical access to food

entities.name: FOOD UTILIZATION
entities.description: The dimension of food security referring to the body's ability to use nutrients
